questions.question: What is the primary goal of 'One-to-Many' repurposing?

options.text: To trick people into thinking you have a huge team
To maximize the reach and life of a single core idea across multiple platforms
To create as many different ideas as possible
To fill up your hard drive with files

questions.explanation: Repurposing ensures that the 5-10 hours you spent on a 'Source Piece' actually reaches the 90% of your audience who missed it the first time you posted.

questions.question: What is a 'Source Piece' in the repurposing workflow?

options.text: A short tweet you post on Monday
A long-form, deeply researched article or video that contains all your main points
A list of links to other people's websites
A guest post you wrote for someone else

questions.explanation: The Source Piece is your 'Master' document. All other social posts, threads, and emails are derived from the research and logic in this one piece.

questions.question: Why is 'Copy-Pasting' the same text across all platforms discouraged?

options.text: It is technically impossible
Each platform has different norms, formatting requirements, and audience expectations
It makes your computer run slower
Google will penalize your website

questions.explanation: A LinkedIn audience expects whitespace and professional stories; a Twitter audience expects punchy lists. Adapting the framing ensures the content feels 'native' to the user.

questions.question: What is a 'Zero-Click' summary?

options.text: A post that has no text and only an image
A content asset that provides all the valuable insights directly in the feed, without requiring a link click
A mistake where a link doesn't work
An ad that no one clicks on

questions.explanation: Zero-click content builds trust and authority by giving value away immediately. It follows the AEO principle of being the 'answer' where the user is already looking.

questions.question: In the recommended Weekly Sequence, what usually happens on Monday?

options.text: The Source Piece is published and emailed
Nothing; you spend the day researching
You post your final 'Hot Take'
You delete your old Twitter threads

questions.explanation: Monday is the 'Launch' day. You publish the heavy-lifting content and notify your most direct audience (your email list) first.

questions.question: Think of one 'Source Piece' title for your business. List 3 specific derivative assets you would create from it. How would you change the framing of the core idea for YOUR primary social platform versus your email newsletter?
questions.promptForAI: Review if the founder understands 'framing.' They should explain a shift in tone or format (e.g., 'Email will be more personal/behind-the-scenes while LinkedIn will focus on the business result').
